TextENGNew: ...How have you been feeling since then?

"Oh, yes.@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2016. Thanks to you... lately I've been feeling good.@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2017. I've been able to sleep well at night now... and I no longer get headaches or sudden bouts of nausea when I'm out walking."
changeReason: New translation omits significant content about sleeping well at night and the absence of headaches/sudden sickness when walking outside.

TextENGNew: Answering the doctor's questions before me, I exhale slowly... and savor the joy of it.
When an adult speaks to you, you look at their face and respond.
...That kind of ordinary, natural gesture was something I was completely incapable of until not so long ago.
And now, I can do it properly, without any difficulty.
Even when my eyes meet the doctor's, my heart doesn't race and my breathing doesn't tighten; my vision doesn't go dark with dizziness.
...I can feel it for myself — a real, solid sense of change.
My 'heart' had grown considerably stronger since I started taking the medicine prescribed a few months ago.

"...Um, thank you so much.@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2019. No matter what treatments I tried before, nothing ever seemed to help...@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2020. But thanks to that medicine, lately it's like the scenery around me has opened up somehow... everything looks brighter."
changeReason: New translation omits dialogue about the medicine making the scenery feel wider and brighter.

TextENGNew: ...Oh, I'm sorry, that came out strangely. I mean, that is to say—

"That's quite all right.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2003. It's easier for me to understand your condition when you speak honestly about what you feel.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2004. ...A wider, brighter field of vision is a positive sign.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2005. It means your mind and heart are gaining the capacity to take in more."
changeReason: New translation omits the psychiatrist's comments about a wider/brighter worldview being a positive sign and proof of mental/emotional capacity.

TextENGNew: A-ahaha...
The laughter that came naturally to my lips surprised me.
It had been so long — truly — since I had laughed without thinking.

changeReason: "...Ah..." captures the vocalized gasp/catch of breath in 「…っ…」 while the original "......" only conveys silence.

TextENGNew: ...After the laughter, tears spilled from my eyes.
I had believed I would have to go on living like this forever, carrying this overcast, suffocating feeling — and that belief had only deepened my despair, pushing it further into the dark.
No matter what I looked at, no matter what I talked about or did, an indescribable exhaustion would envelope my whole body... to the point where at times I had nearly lost the will to live.
But now... that heavy, suffocating curse is slowly fading from within me.
changeReason: "curse" accurately translates 呪縛 (curse/binding spell) while "baggage" conveys a fundamentally different metaphor.

TextENGNew: In front of eyes that had seen nothing but darkness, a small but certain light has at last appeared...

"Now, now, don't cry.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2007. You worked so hard over these past two months.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2008. Once your heart recovers its strength, I'm sure you'll be able to return to a normal life."
changeReason: New translation omits the psychiatrist's assurance that once her heart heals she can return to normal life.

TextENGNew: So now... smile, all right? Just a little more — no rush, no hurry — let's keep working through it together.
Yes... yes!
The doctor said this, and gently rubbed my back as I shook with sobs.
Warm, soft... so very gentle.
Thank goodness... truly, thank goodness...!
I was anxious at first — but I made the decision to take the medicine this person recommended...
changeReason: New correctly uses gender-neutral "this person" matching この人, while old assumed female with "she."

TextENGNew: "...Yes.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2012. The junior high student patient I mentioned — her recovery has been truly remarkable.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2013. Her blood test results have come very close to normal, and her pulse and perspiration levels have stabilized as well."
changeReason: New translation omits specific medical details about blood test results approaching normal and stabilized heart rate and sweat rate.

TextENGNew: "At first, even a somewhat probing question during examination would send her into a fit... but recently she has been able to comprehend her own thought processes with considerable accuracy, and her conversation shows clear coherence."

"She still seems to struggle slightly with regulating her emotional fluctuations, but her psychological tests are no longer showing extreme results... I believe returning her to daily life in the near future would pose no significant problems."
"No, no — my counseling has been entirely standard, nothing special. It really is the experimental drug you introduced me to, Doctor. That makes all the difference."

``Even so... this is the fourth patient we've conducted clinical trials on using this experimental drug, and if three of them including her manage to reintegrate into society, that's sufficient proof of efficacy.``
changeReason: Old says 'other two patients who managed social reintegration'; new says 'three including her.' JPN 彼女を合わせて３人 = three including her. Nuanced counting difference.

TextENGNew: "Physician colleagues of mine have been impressed by how well it works... at this rate, once the clinical data from this batch is gathered, even the cautious members of the Pharmaceutical Affairs Council should be convinced."
...Hm? The one remaining patient's response?

"Well... it isn't that it has no effect, but there are some slightly concerning symptoms, so I plan to confirm their physical and mental state at the time of administration before conducting another dose."

"I believe it was most likely a case caused by concurrent use with another drug... but depending on the situation, I may need to submit the clinical results as a flagged alert to the pharmaceutical company."
"...Ah, of course, those records will be excluded from the regular clinical data."

"...No, that report is not something I can relay over the phone just yet.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2026. It happened too suddenly, and at this point I cannot determine whether it was a side effect of this drug or not... Could you give me a little more time?"
changeReason: New translation omits the request for more time (もう少し、お時間をいただけますか).

TextENGNew: "Should the worst come to pass, you will be the first I inform. ...Well, then."
changeReason: "Well, then" correctly translates the farewell それでは, while "Thanks" is a mistranslation of this closing.

TextENGNew: ``........@k@|@y@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2030.Chemically speaking, it absolutely shouldn't be possible.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_menD2031. Seeing hallucinations after taking it, that's...``
